description:
A data-science colleague left you a raw sensor log at:
        /home/user/data/sensor_readings_2023.log
        
        1. Inspect the file structure.  
           • Every line is an ISO-8601 time-stamp followed by a space-separated list of key=value pairs.  
           • Lines whose key STATUS has the value FAIL mark malfunction events that must be isolated.  
        
        2. Create a new directory called /home/user/output (if it is not already present).

        3. Using a single regular-expression pipeline (you may chain standard GNU/Linux text-processing utilities
           such as grep, awk, sed, etc.), extract **only** the lines whose STATUS field equals FAIL, preserving
           their original order and **without** introducing or deleting whitespace.

           • Save the exact matching lines in a new UTF-8 text file located at
             /home/user/output/failures_2023.log

        4. Produce a one-line summary file called
           /home/user/output/failures_count.txt  
           – The file must contain **only** the decimal count of lines found in step 3, followed by a single
             trailing newline character (LF, ASCII 0x0A).  
           – No additional spaces or text are allowed.

        Verification details the grader will check:
        • The directory /home/user/output exists.  
        • /home/user/output/failures_2023.log exists and contains exclusively the malfunction lines, in the same
          order as they appear in the original log.  
        • /home/user/output/failures_count.txt exists and contains exactly the line-count of the malfunction
          lines, nothing else.  
        
        You do not need root privileges.  Use only standard utilities available in a typical minimal Linux
        environment.  Display any intermediate console output you consider helpful; the automated tests will look
        only at the resulting files for grading.  A correct solution should require no more than two or three
        shell commands in total.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing filesystem state -->
        Directories:
          /home/user/data               (already present)
        Files:
          /home/user/data/sensor_readings_2023.log  (UTF-8 text, 10 lines):

          2023-06-01T00:00:01Z SENSOR_ID=alpha STATUS=OK   TEMP=24.5C HUM=45%
          2023-06-01T00:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=alpha STATUS=FAIL TEMP=0.0C  HUM=0%
          2023-06-01T01:00:01Z SENSOR_ID=beta  STATUS=OK   TEMP=23.1C HUM=47%
          2023-06-01T01:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=beta  STATUS=FAIL TEMP=-5.0C HUM=0%
          2023-06-01T02:00:01Z SENSOR_ID=gamma STATUS=OK   TEMP=22.8C HUM=44%
          2023-06-01T02:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=gamma STATUS=OK   TEMP=22.9C HUM=44%
          2023-06-01T03:00:01Z SENSOR_ID=alpha STATUS=OK   TEMP=24.7C HUM=45%
          2023-06-01T03:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=beta  STATUS=OK   TEMP=23.4C HUM=47%
          2023-06-01T04:00:01Z SENSOR_ID=gamma STATUS=FAIL TEMP=-10.0C HUM=0%
          2023-06-01T04:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=alpha STATUS=OK   TEMP=24.6C HUM=46%

        <!-- Expected artefacts after agent completes the task -->
        Directories that must exist:
          /home/user/output                   (permissions 755 or more permissive)

        Files that must exist:

          1. /home/user/output/failures_2023.log  (exactly 3 lines, contents:)

             2023-06-01T00:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=alpha STATUS=FAIL TEMP=0.0C  HUM=0%
             2023-06-01T01:30:01Z SENSOR_ID=beta  STATUS=FAIL TEMP=-5.0C HUM=0%
             2023-06-01T04:00:01Z SENSOR_ID=gamma STATUS=FAIL TEMP=-10.0C HUM=0%

             • Each line ends with a single LF newline.
             • No extra blank lines at start or end.

          2. /home/user/output/failures_count.txt  (exactly one line, content:)

             3\n                             (# ASCII “3”, then LF newline)

        The grading script will confirm:
        • The filtered log contains those three lines and nothing else, preserving exact byte sequence including
          internal spacing.  
        • The count file contains the single character “3” followed by a newline and no other bytes.
